events.name: Carrington Event
events.description: The most intense geomagnetic storm in recorded history. Auroras visible near equator. Telegraph systems worldwide failed.
events.notes: White-light flare observed by Richard Carrington on Sept 1, CME arrived ~17 hours later

events.name: Halloween Storms
events.description: Series of massive solar flares including the 3rd largest on record. Damaged satellites, disrupted power grids.
events.notes: Multiple X-class flares from AR 486, including X17 and X10+ events

events.name: Quebec Blackout
events.description: 6 million people lost power in Quebec for 9 hours. Aurora seen as far south as Texas.
events.notes: CME from AR 5395, collapsed Hydro-Quebec power grid in 90 seconds

events.name: Bastille Day Event
events.description: Major solar eruption coinciding with French national holiday. Proton storm disrupted satellites.
events.notes: Full-halo CME, produced spectacular auroras globally

events.name: Miyake Event 774 CE
events.description: Massive cosmic ray spike detected in tree rings. Likely 10x stronger than Carrington. Origin unknown.
events.notes: Carbon-14 spike suggests superflare or nearby gamma-ray burst

events.name: New York Railroad Storm
events.description: Major geomagnetic storm disrupted telegraph and railroad signals. Aurora visible in tropics.
events.notes: Brewster NY railroad station caught fire from induced currents

events.name: Valentine's Day Storm
events.description: First major flare of Solar Cycle 24. Disrupted radio communications in China.
events.notes: AR 1158 produced multiple M-class and X-class flares

events.name: Apollo 16 Near-Miss
events.description: Massive solar storm occurred between Apollo 16 and 17 missions. Would have been lethal to astronauts.
events.notes: Fastest CME on record at the time, 2850 km/s

events.name: July 2012 Near-Miss
events.description: Carrington-class CME missed Earth by 9 days. Would have caused $2 trillion in damage.
events.notes: Fastest CME since 1859, tracked by STEREO spacecraft

events.name: Starlink Loss Event
events.description: 40 Starlink satellites destroyed by atmospheric expansion from minor geomagnetic storm.
